req.title: Read patient
req.statement: A clinician shall read a patient resource for patients in their care; reading outside their assignment shall be denied.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-002 enforces access control on patient read operations: only assigned clinicians may retrieve patient resources, others receive 403. The test suite covers: (1) positive case—assigned clinician reads assigned patient (EP, success path); (2) negative case—unassigned clinician reads patient (NEG, explicit denial); (3) boundary case—clinician at assignment boundary transitions from assigned to unassigned state (STC, state change). All tests validate HTTP status, response structure, and audit trail presence where applicable.

tests.name: test_clinician_reads_assigned_patient_success
preconditions: Clinician with ID 'CL-001' exists in role 'clinician'
Assignment record exists: clinician CL-001 assigned to patient PT-0042
Valid bearer token for clinician CL-001 available
actions: Set Authorization header with clinician CL-001 bearer token
expected: Response body contains Patient resource with id='PT-0042'
Response includes resourceType field set to 'Patient'
Audit log entry created with action='read', actor='CL-001', resource='PT-0042', outcome='success'
trace: R-HLT-002: A clinician shall read a patient resource for patients in their care
R-HLT-002 expected outcomes: resource returned; access audit written
tests.executable:
import requests
import json
from datetime import datetime

def test_clinician_reads_assigned_patient_success():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    clinician_token = <get_clinician_token('CL-001')>
    patient_id = 'PT-0042'
    
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {clinician_token}'}
    resp = requests.get(f'{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}', headers=headers)
    
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f'Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}'
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get('resourceType') == 'Patient'
    assert body.get('id') == patient_id
    assert 'name' in body or 'identifier' in body
    
    audit = <query_audit_log('CL-001', patient_id)>
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit['action'] == 'read'
    assert audit['outcome'] == 'success'

tests.name: test_unassigned_clinician_denied_patient_read
preconditions: Clinician with ID 'CL-002' exists in role 'clinician'
NO assignment record exists between clinician CL-002 and patient PT-0099
Valid bearer token for clinician CL-002 available
actions: Set Authorization header with clinician CL-002 bearer token
expected: Response body contains error code or message indicating access denied
Audit log entry created with action='read', actor='CL-002', resource='PT-0099', outcome='denied'
No patient resource payload in response body
trace: R-HLT-002: reading outside their assignment shall be denied
R-HLT-002 error pathways: 403 when clinician not assigned
tests.executable:
import requests
import json

def test_unassigned_clinician_denied_patient_read():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    clinician_token = <get_clinician_token('CL-002')>
    patient_id = 'PT-0099'
    
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {clinician_token}'}
    resp = requests.get(f'{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}', headers=headers)
    
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f'Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}'
    body = resp.json()
    assert 'error' in body or 'issue' in body
    assert body.get('resourceType') != 'Patient'
    
    audit = <query_audit_log('CL-002', patient_id)>
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit['action'] == 'read'
    assert audit['outcome'] == 'denied'

tests.name: test_clinician_assignment_revocation_boundary
preconditions: Clinician with ID 'CL-003' exists in role 'clinician'
Active assignment record exists: clinician CL-003 assigned to patient PT-0050
Valid bearer token for clinician CL-003 available
System supports assignment revocation via API
actions: First read: Send GET /Patient/PT-0050 with CL-003 token (should succeed)
Revoke assignment: Call admin endpoint to remove CL-003 → PT-0050 assignment
Second read: Send GET /Patient/PT-0050 with CL-003 token (should fail)
expected: First read returns HTTP 200 with Patient resource
Audit log contains two read entries: first with outcome='success', second with outcome='denied'
State transition from 'assigned' to 'unassigned' is recorded
trace: R-HLT-002: State transition coverage—clinician moves from assigned to unassigned state
R-HLT-002 boundary: permission changes at assignment revocation event
tests.executable:
import requests
import time

def test_clinician_assignment_revocation_boundary():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    admin_token = <get_admin_token()>
    clinician_token = <get_clinician_token('CL-003')>
    patient_id = 'PT-0050'
    clinician_id = 'CL-003'
    
    headers_clinician = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {clinician_token}'}
    headers_admin = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {admin_token}'}
    
    resp1 = requests.get(f'{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}', headers=headers_clinician)
    assert resp1.status_code == 200
    
    revoke_resp = requests.delete(
        f'{base_url}/Assignment/{clinician_id}/{patient_id}',
        headers=headers_admin
    )
    assert revoke_resp.status_code in [200, 204]
    time.sleep(0.5)
    
    resp2 = requests.get(f'{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}', headers=headers_clinician)
    assert resp2.status_code == 403
